title: Human Body Systems
description: Major organ systems, organs, and their functions

cards.back: Transports blood, oxygen, and nutrients throughout the body

cards.front: System that transports blood and oxygen
cards.back: Circulatory system
Heart
Pumps blood through the body; has four chambers
Left atrium, right atrium, left ventricle, right ventricle
Arteries carry blood away from the heart; veins carry blood toward the heart

cards.front: Carry blood away from the heart
cards.back: Arteries
Veins
Tiny blood vessels where oxygen/nutrient exchange occurs with tissues
Carry oxygen using hemoglobin
Fight infection and disease (immune system)
Help blood clot to stop bleeding
Brings oxygen into the body and removes carbon dioxide
Respiratory system
Lungs, trachea, bronchi, diaphragm
Muscle that contracts to help lungs expand for breathing
Tiny air sacs in the lungs where oxygen and CO₂ are exchanged
Breaks down food into nutrients the body can absorb
Digestive system
Mouth, esophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine
Where most nutrient absorption occurs
Small intestine
Absorbs water; forms and stores waste (feces)
Filters blood, produces bile, processes nutrients and toxins
Produces insulin and digestive enzymes
Controls body functions through electrical signals; coordinates responses

cards.front: System that sends electrical signals to control the body
cards.back: Nervous system
Brain, spinal cord, nerves
Largest part of the brain; controls thinking, memory, speech, and voluntary movement
Controls balance, coordination, and fine motor skills
Controls automatic functions like breathing, heart rate, and digestion
A nerve cell that transmits electrical signals
The gap between two neurons where signals are transmitted chemically
Supports the body, protects organs, allows movement, produces blood cells

cards.front: How many bones in the adult human body?

cards.back: Femur (thigh bone)
Stapes (in the ear)
Enables movement, maintains posture, generates heat
Skeletal (voluntary), smooth (involuntary), cardiac (heart)
Produces hormones that regulate growth, metabolism, and reproduction
Endocrine system
Master gland — controls other endocrine glands; regulates growth
Regulates metabolism, energy, and growth
Produce adrenaline (fight-or-flight) and cortisol (stress response)
Pancreas
Defends the body against pathogens (bacteria, viruses, etc.)
Immune system
Proteins produced by white blood cells to fight specific pathogens
Removes waste products from the blood; produces urine
Filter blood to remove waste and excess water, producing urine
Skin, hair, nails — protects the body, regulates temperature
Skin
Produces offspring; involves gametes (egg and sperm)
